subject: Re: Customer Service Department Budget Planning FY 2025
body:
Hi Peter,

Thank you for your input on the budget for the next fiscal year. I've reviewed the figures and it looks like we're on the same page. Let's finalize the budget so we can prepare for the upcoming financial year.

Best,
Rachel

subject: Weekly Team Meeting Agenda
body:
Good morning team,

The weekly team meeting will take place tomorrow at 10 AM. We'll discuss the current projects and any outstanding issues. Please prepare any updates from your respective areas before the meeting.

Best,
Rachel

subject: New Training Materials for the Virtual Care Platform
body:
Hi Rachel,

I've completed the new training materials for the virtual care platform. I believe this will be useful for our new hires and current employees who need a refresher. Please take a look when you have a moment.

Best,
Mark

subject: Re: Annual Performance Reviews
body:
Hi Susan,

I've received the performance reviews for the customer service team. I'll take some time to go through them and provide my feedback. We can schedule a meeting next week to discuss further.

Best,
Rachel

subject: FY24 - Customer Retention Analysis
body:
Hi Rachel,

As per your request, we have analyzed the customer retention numbers for FY24. From the data, we found a significant insight that you might find interesting. According to the data, 87% of patients who contacted our customer service remained active users after six months, compared to 82% who never contacted support.

This could suggest that our customer service is playing a crucial role in retaining customers and possibly even enhancing their experience with MediConn. We should consider this while planning strategies to reduce service calls in Q12025. 

Let me know if you need anything else.

Best,
Peter

subject: Re: FY24 - Customer Retention Analysis
body:
Hi Peter,

Thanks for sharing these insights. It seems our customer service is indeed valuable to our patients. While we aim for fewer service calls, we should ensure that we maintain this level of patient communication and satisfaction.

I'll factor this into our strategies for Q12025. Let's discuss more in our upcoming meeting.

Best,
Rachel

subject: Re: FY24 - Customer Retention Analysis
body:
Hello Rachel and Peter,

I agree with the points made. Our customer service is indeed crucial in retaining patients, and it's something we should consider while making plans to reduce service calls.

I look forward to discussing this further in our meeting.

Best,
Susan
